methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Summit Nanotech, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Amanda Hall, has raised approximately C$120M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a comparable company set comprising one public peer (ticker: LNZA) and eight private companies operating in the same sector and stage, including Graphyte, Fourth Power, Steady Energy, TAE Technologies, and Redwood Materials.

The peer-median method serves as the primary valuation triangle. Analysis of three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This method directly reflects market pricing for Series A climate technology businesses of similar maturity and risk profile.

The triangulation analysis, constrained by the availability of a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, with a median point estimate of $150M. This implies a post-money valuation consistent with peer precedent. Key valuation uncertainties remain material: the composition and performance characteristics of the broader eight-company private peer set are not detailed, and public market comparables (represented by a single ticker) may not fully capture the risk and stage-specific discount typically applied to private Series A businesses. A more granular analysis of the full peer set and relative metrics would strengthen confidence in this range.
